example_scripts.script:
There was a time… when hearing someone's voice… meant everything.
Back then… people waited hours… sometimes days… just for a call.
Not because it was easy… but because it mattered.
And now… They silence calls… from the people who care the most.
Maybe… They didn't lose connection… They just stopped valuing it.

Sunday used to feel like the world agreed to pause.
Everyone gathered… not because they were summoned… but because they wanted to be there.
The table wasn't fancy… but every seat was filled.
Now Sundays come and go… and most people spend them alone with a screen.
We have more freedom than ever… and nowhere we truly want to be.

Remember when you could tell someone everything… without saying a word?
A glance across a room used to carry the weight of a whole conversation.
People knew how to look at each other… really look.
Now we stare at glass… and call it connection.
The saddest part… is we don't even notice what we've stopped seeing.

Someone used to wait at the door when you came home.
Not because they had to… but because your arrival made the room feel complete.
That kind of love never announced itself… it just stood there, quietly.
These days we walk into empty houses and call them peaceful.
But peace and loneliness… sound exactly the same in an empty hallway.

Once… someone stayed up three nights making something just for you.
They didn't have money… so they gave you time instead.
That was the point — not the thing itself, but the hours it held.
Now we click a button and wonder why it doesn't feel the same.
You can't order the feeling of being someone's whole thought for three days.

visual_keywords: two kids riding side by side
example_scripts.script:
There was a version of you… who had nowhere to be and didn't care.
You rode until the streetlights came on… and even then, just a little longer.
The world was small enough to be whole back then.
Now you pass those same streets in a car… and don't even look up.
Growing up is just learning to stop noticing what you've lost.
